title: Prunus × iranshahrii
intro: Prunus × iranshahrii is a hybrid cherry tree species known for its unique characteristics and potential health benefits. The health benefits of Prunus × iranshahrii are attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Prunus × iranshahrii is commonly used in traditional medicine and can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Prunus × iranshahrii is a hybrid of Prunus avium and Prunus cerasus, which are both species of cherry. This hybrid is commonly referred to as the Iranian cherry. Its classification is that of a deciduous shrub or small tree, belonging to the family Rosaceae. Prunus × iranshahrii is native to Iran, where it has been cultivated for its fruit, which is edible and rich in antioxidants. The classification of Prunus × iranshahrii reflects its unique characteristics as a cross between two distinct species of cherry.
uses: Prunus × iranshahrii is a hybrid cherry species, but information on its specific medicinal uses is limited.
